commands.author: scotthuang

target.title: fix(node-host): restart stale node host on version mismatch
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: After `openclaw update`, the `openclaw-node` launchd service keeps running with stale in-memory ESM modules. When the Browser tool (or any lazy-imported path) triggers a dynamic import, it reads new chunk files from disk whose export names don't match the old module graph, causing `SyntaxError: does not provide an export named 'r'`.
- Why it matters: Browser tool and other node-host-routed tools silently break after every package update until the user manually discovers they need to `openclaw node restart`. Note: same-version rebuilds (`pnpm build` without a version bump) are not covered by this check—that scenario requires build-identity comparison and is tracked separately.
- What changed: Gateway now compares its own version against the node host's reported `clientVersion` on connect. The check fires **only** when all of: (1) connection is from loopback (`isLocalClient`), (2) the node's `instanceId` matches the local `~/.openclaw/node.json` nodeId (proving same-install), (3) both versions are production release format (`YYYY.M.D`), and (4) versions differ. On match, it closes the connection with a new `CLIENT_VERSION_MISMATCH` detail code. The `GatewayClient` reconnect-pause predicate includes this code so that `onReconnectPaused` fires on reconnect, causing the node host to `exit(1)` so the OS supervisor (launchd/systemd) restarts it with fresh code. Remote nodes are exempt—both non-loopback connections and SSH-tunneled loopback connections (different instanceId) are never kicked (#83736). The check is placed **before** `setClient()`/`upsertPresence()` so rejected nodes never appear online.
- Why local nodes are always safe to kick: A local node host runs from the same package installation as the gateway. The version check now additionally verifies same-install by comparing `connectParams.client.instanceId` against the gateway's local `~/.openclaw/node.json` nodeId. SSH-tunneled remote nodes appear as loopback but have different instanceIds, so they are never mistaken for the local same-install node. When `openclaw update` replaces the dist directory, both gateway and local node share the same on-disk code. The gateway restarts first, and the local node host is guaranteed to pick up the new version on its next restart—the fresh code is already on disk.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): Remote/subordinate nodes are not kicked on version mismatch—neither non-loopback connections nor SSH-tunneled loopback connections (which have different instanceIds). Operator UI clients are not version-checked. Gateway-to-gateway protocol versioning is untouched. The existing `PROTOCOL_MISMATCH` code remains for wire-protocol negotiation.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related #83736 (gateway should tolerate node version skew for remote nodes—this PR respects that by only kicking local nodes)
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: `openclaw-node` is a long-lived launchd service with `KeepAlive=true`. It only exits on auth errors. Neither `openclaw update` nor `openclaw gateway restart` restart the node service. After dist files are replaced, the running process holds old modules in memory but lazy dynamic imports read new chunk files from disk, causing ESM static-analysis link failures.
- Missing detection / guardrail: No version consistency check between gateway and connected node hosts. Additionally, the `GatewayClient` reconnect-pause predicate had no entry for version-mismatch detail codes, so even if the gateway rejected a stale node, the client would auto-reconnect indefinitely.
- Contributing context (if known): npm atomic package replacement doesn't crash the running Node.js process (already-loaded ESM modules stay in memory), so launchd never detects a failure to trigger restart.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `src/node-host/runner.credentials.test.ts` — node host exit behavior on `CLIENT_VERSION_MISMATCH`
  - `src/gateway/server.node-version-mismatch.test.ts` — gateway handshake-level coverage
- Scenarios locked in:
  - Local node with matching released version → allowed
  - Local node with mismatched released version → rejected with `CLIENT_VERSION_MISMATCH`
  - Local node with dev/test version (`"dev"`, `"1.0.0"`) → allowed (non-release format skips guard)
  - Remote mismatch → allowed (verified by code review: `isLocalClient` gate; no integration test since test server binds 127.0.0.1 which is always local)
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: Node-host exit test covers the critical recovery path. Gateway seam test covers the version comparison and `isReleasedVersion` logic at handshake time without a full multi-machine setup.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): `src/gateway/server.node-pairing-authz.test.ts` indirectly proves dev versions (`"1.0.0"`) are not kicked.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

After a package update, the node host automatically restarts within ~1 second (launchd `ThrottleInterval=1`) instead of silently serving stale code until manually restarted.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
npm install -g openclaw -> gateway restarts -> local node host reconnects with old code -> lazy import fails -> INVALID_REQUEST error

After (local node, same machine, package version changed):
npm install -g openclaw -> gateway restarts -> local node host reconnects -> gateway sees isLocalClient + released version mismatch -> close(1008, CLIENT_VERSION_MISMATCH) -> GatewayClient pauses reconnect -> onReconnectPaused -> handleNodeHostReconnectPaused -> exit(1) -> launchd restarts -> fresh code loaded -> success

Remote node (different machine, may not have updated yet):
gateway restarts -> remote node reconnects with old version -> gateway allows it (isLocalClient=false) -> node stays connected, works with available capabilities

Not covered (same version, different build):
pnpm build (no version bump) -> gateway restarts -> node host reconnects -> versions match -> no kick -> stale code may still cause ESM errors (requires build-identity check, tracked separately)
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS (launchd)
- Runtime/container: Node v25.8.1
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Browser tool (any node-host-routed tool)
- Relevant config (redacted): `ai.openclaw.node` launchd plist with `KeepAlive=true`

### Steps

1. Have `openclaw-node` service running (installed via `openclaw node install`)
2. Update openclaw (`npm install -g openclaw@latest`)
3. Gateway auto-restarts with new version
4. Call a Browser tool action (e.g. `browser snapshot`)

### Expected

- Browser tool works normally after update

### Actual

- `INVALID_REQUEST: SyntaxError: The requested module './synthetic-auth.runtime.js' does not provide an export named 'r'`

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets

### Unit tests

- `src/node-host/runner.credentials.test.ts`: 9 passed (including new `CLIENT_VERSION_MISMATCH` exit test)
- `src/gateway/server.node-pairing-authz.test.ts`: 5 passed (previously failed with `GatewayClientRequestError: client version mismatch` before adding `isReleasedVersion` guard)
- `src/gateway/server.node-version-mismatch.test.ts`: 4 passed (gateway handshake: mismatch rejected, dev allowed, matching allowed, semver allowed)

Type check: zero errors. Lint (oxlint): clean.

### Manual end-to-end verification

Gateway started from source dist with `OPENCLAW_VERSION=2099.1.1` override; node host started from same dist (reports real version `2026.5.25`):

**Gateway log (stdout):**
```
2026-05-25T22:36:29.172+08:00 [ws] node version mismatch conn=0073e848-9ee0-46a0-8fc5-a150637d5d50 client=SCOTTHUANG-MB1 clientVersion=2026.5.25 gatewayVersion=2099.1.1; closing for supervisor restart
```

**Node host log (stderr):**
```
node host gateway connect failed: client version mismatch
gateway connect failed: GatewayClientRequestError: client version mismatch
node host gateway closed (1008): client version mismatch
```

Full chain confirmed: gateway detects mismatch → sends `CLIENT_VERSION_MISMATCH` detail → close(1008) → node host receives and exits. In production, launchd `KeepAlive=true` restarts the process with fresh code.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: Local `openclaw-node` launchd service no longer silently runs stale code after a package update. Gateway kicks the mismatched node host on reconnect, triggering a clean supervisor restart.
- Real environment tested: macOS 15.5 arm64, Node v25.8.1, OpenClaw built from source (`pnpm build`), gateway started with `OPENCLAW_VERSION=2099.1.1` override to simulate version drift, node host reports real built version `2026.5.25`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  1. Built source with fix: `pnpm build`
  2. Started gateway from source dist with fake version: `OPENCLAW_VERSION=2099.1.1 node dist/index.js gateway --port 19999`
  3. Started node host from same dist (real version): `node dist/index.js node run --host 127.0.0.1 --port 19999`
  4. Observed node host stderr and gateway log for version mismatch handling
- Evidence after fix:
  - Gateway stdout: `2026-05-25T22:36:29.172+08:00 [ws] node version mismatch conn=0073e848-9ee0-46a0-8fc5-a150637d5d50 client=SCOTTHUANG-MB1 clientVersion=2026.5.25 gatewayVersion=2099.1.1; closing for supervisor restart`
  - Node host stderr: `node host gateway connect failed: client version mismatch` / `gateway connect failed: GatewayClientRequestError: client version mismatch` / `node host gateway closed (1008): client version mismatch`
- Observed result after fix: Node host process exited after receiving version mismatch close. In production this triggers launchd restart with fresh code from the updated dist.
- What was not tested: Full launchd restart cycle end-to-end (would require two sequential package installs); remote node exemption (test server binds 127.0.0.1, always local); beta version format (`2026.5.25-beta.1`).

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: Unit test confirms `CLIENT_VERSION_MISMATCH` triggers exit(1); gateway pairing test passes (dev/test clients not kicked); `GatewayClient.shouldPauseReconnectAfterAuthFailure` includes the new code so reconnect-pause fires correctly; type-check passes; confirmed the running `openclaw-node` process was stale (started May 23, dist rebuilt May 24-25).
- Edge cases checked: Both versions must be truthy AND match `YYYY.M.D` release format for the check to fire. Dev sentinels (`"dev"`, `"1.0.0"`, `"unknown"`) are exempt. Remote clients (`isLocalClient=false`) are exempt. Rejection happens before `setClient`/`upsertPresence` so no phantom presence. Reconnect path pauses (not just initial connect rejection).
- What you did **not** verify: Full end-to-end with actual launchd restart cycle (would require two package versions installed sequentially).

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Local node host gets kicked on every reconnect during a brief window where gateway has restarted but node host hasn't yet (restart loop until versions align).
  - Mitigation: launchd `ThrottleInterval=1` means restart is near-instant (~1s). After one restart the versions match and the loop stops. The exit code path is the same as existing auth-error exits which are already proven stable with launchd supervision.
- Risk: SSH-tunneled remote nodes mistaken for local and kicked.
  - Mitigation: The check requires `instanceId === localNodeId` (read from `~/.openclaw/node.json`). SSH-tunneled remote nodes have different instanceIds so they never match, even though they appear as loopback. Only the node installed via `openclaw node install` on the same machine shares this nodeId.
target.author: scotthuang

commands.reason: matching ClawSweeper response comment already exists
